channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Social media addicts: Here are tips to stop wasting time on apps
item.description: If you’ve got a smartphone, you probably spend too much time on it — checking Instagram, watching silly TikTok videos, messaging on WhatsApp or doomscrolling on X. It can be hard to curb excessive use of smartphones and social media, which are addictive by design.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Senate Banking panel advances stablecoin bill
item.description: The Senate Banking Committee voted Thursday to advanced legislation to create a regulatory framework for payment stablecoins.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Toyota's self-charging concept EV could help you tackle the daily commute on solar power alone
item.description: Toyota's new micro mobility concept has a solar roof that it says can generate 20 miles of range per day.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The WikiPortraits Project: Bringing Dignity to Celebrity Wikipedia Pages
item.description: WikiPortraits is a team of volunteer photographers dedicated to improving the quality and accuracy of celebrity Wikipedia pages through high-quality, freely licensed images.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: What CIOs need to secure beyond tech to successfully transform
item.description: Digital transformation technologies alone don’t make digital transformation. CIOs know this well, even when they increase their IT investments.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Vaccinating poultry could help cut soaring egg prices but US remains hesitant
item.description: Vaccines could be a key means of suppressing bird flu and avoiding the slaughter of millions of chickens, which is blamed for egg prices averaging nearly $5 a dozen.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gov tech layoffs could hamper SHARE IT Act software mandates
item.description: The SHARE IT Act requires agencies to share custom-developed software code. But the termination of 18F and other tech experts could hamper the law's goals. The post Gov tech layoffs could hamper SHARE IT Act software mandates first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Chinese Cyberespionage Group Targets Juniper MX Series Routers
item.description: Chinese cyberespionage group targets Juniper MX series routers, exploiting proprietary network-edge devices and developing custom malware.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: OpenAI takes on rivals with new Responses API, Agents SDK
item.description: OpenAI’s new Responses API and an upgraded Agents SDK will help enterprises more easily build agents with advanced reasoning and multimodal capabilities, the company said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cato Networks unveils next-gen firewall
item.description: Cato Networks this week introduced its next-generation firewall as part of its secure access service edge (SASE) platform, saying the NGFW would mitigate security vulnerabilities left unpatched by manual efforts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Supercharging your cybersecurity strategy with AI
item.description: Artificial intelligence has been unleashed on the world. While its power is undeniable, its full potential to wreak havoc is still unknown. Cyber-criminals have few concerns about AI running amok, because chaos furthers their goals.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Laser-wielding robots are redefining farming
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At long last, OpenStack (now known as OpenInfra Foundation) joins Linux Foundation
item.description: After more than a decade as an independent open-source foundation, the OpenStack project is joining the Linux Foundation in a move aimed at accelerating collaboration across the open infrastructure ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Finally: some truth serum for lying genAI chatbots
item.description: Ever since OpenAI made ChatGPT available to the public in late 2022, the large language model (LLM)-based generative AI (genAI) revolution has advanced at an astonishing pace. Two years and four months ago, we had only ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Go-based TypeScript to dramatically improve speed, scalability
item.description: Microsoft is developing a native TypeScript implementation based on Google’s Go language. The initiative promises dramatic improvements in editor startup speed, build times, and memory usage, making it easier to scale TypeScript to large code bases, Microsoft said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2
item.description: Joe Welch, the deputy to the commanding general of the Army Futures Command, said the characteristics of need helps define the problem set for industry. The post How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride
item.description: This blog will focus on requirements in the context of the federal government. Sound requirements drive best value pricing and performance for customer agencies The post Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network
